$comment: SEC-4.b: diff of the permissions granted by `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json` against the call-site inventory in `src-tauri/capabilities/inventory.json` (SEC-4.a). Every string in `over_permissive` appears in `default.json`'s `permissions` array but does NOT appear in `inventory.json`'s `permissions_used`. SEC-4.c (issue #190) will consume this artifact to prune `default.json` to a least-privilege set. NOTE: this is the asymmetric diff in one direction only (granted - used); the inverse case (used but not granted) is out of scope for SEC-4.b â€” see the `notes` section for the audit trail.

rationale.dialog:default: The `@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog` import surface in `src/` only ever calls `save()` (per inventory.json plugin_imports). The narrower `dialog:allow-save` permission â€” already granted in default.json â€” fully covers that call site. `dialog:default` bundles `allow-ask`, `allow-confirm`, `allow-message`, `allow-open`, and `allow-save`; the first four are not invoked anywhere and re-adding `dialog:default` would only be required if a future change re-introduced ask/confirm/message/open dialogs.
rationale.opener:default: Only `openUrl()` is invoked, from `src/routes/unternehmen/+page.svelte:2` (per inventory.json plugin_imports). The narrower `opener:allow-open-url` covers that call exactly. `opener:default` additionally bundles `allow-open-path`, `allow-open-url`, `allow-reveal-item-in-dir`, etc. â€” re-adding `opener:default` would only be required if SPA code starts calling `openPath()` or `revealItemInDir()`.
rationale.sql:default: The SQL plugin call sites in `src/lib/db/*` only use `Database.load()`, `.select()`, and `.execute()` (per inventory.json sql_plugin_methods_used + plugin_imports for `Database`). Those map to `sql:allow-load`, `sql:allow-select`, and `sql:allow-execute`. `sql:default` is a superset that also bundles `allow-close`. Since the SPA never explicitly closes the SQLite handle (it lives for the lifetime of the renderer), `sql:default` adds no exercised coverage.

notes.scope: This artifact is the asymmetric diff `default.json.permissions \ inventory.json.permissions_used`. The inverse diff (permissions exercised by the SPA but missing from default.json) is intentionally NOT computed here â€” SEC-4.b's verification rule is only the over-permissive direction.
notes.inverse_diff_audit_trail: For reviewer reference, `inventory.json.permissions_used` contains three permissions that are not literally present as strings in `default.json.permissions`: `core:window:allow-close`, `opener:allow-open-url`, and `sql:allow-load`. These are reachable today only because the `*:default` aggregates in `default.json` (`core:default`, `opener:default`, `sql:default`) transitively grant them. When SEC-4.c prunes the `*:default` aggregates, those three narrower permissions must be added explicitly so the SPA keeps working.
notes.core_default_kept: `core:default` is NOT in the over-permissive set: per Tauri v2 semantics, all custom `#[tauri::command]` handlers registered via `generate_handler!` are gated by `core:default`, and inventory.json's `commands` array lists 17 such invocations spread across the SPA. Pruning `core:default` would break every custom-command call site.
